records.ety: [L. rapere, raptum, to carry off by force. See Rapid.]

defs: A seizing by violence; a hurrying along; rapidity with violence.
quotes: That 'gainst a rock, or flat, her keel did dash With headlong rapture.

defs: The state or condition of being rapt, or carried away from one's self by agreeable excitement; violence of a pleasing passion; extreme joy or pleasure; ecstasy.
quotes: Music, when thus applied, raises in the mind of the hearer great conceptions; it strengthens devotion, and advances praise into rapture.
You grow correct that once with rapture writ.

defs: A spasm; a fit; a syncope; delirium.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Raptured (-trd; 135); p. pr. & vb. n. Rapturing.]

defs: To transport with excitement; to enrapture.
